mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts short-term equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5d SPX returns in more than 70% of observations during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low policy rates combined with declining unemployment create a favorable liquidity and earnings environment that forces equity prices higher, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 20-day horizons or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by more than 2 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized confirms sustained economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0 percent is followed by negative 60d SPX returns in more than 60% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor market expansion that fuels wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with 20d SPX direction with a rank correlation coefficient below 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation alongside stagnant industrial production triggers expectations of restrictive monetary policy and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: High CPIAUCSL combined with flat INDPRO results in positive 5d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases.
